reviews.review_title: Not as good as the steam version
reviews.review_description: This app struggles with folder depth, so forget sorting your music  in folders. Could you at least add support for Nas or shared storage? This is the least you could do for us

reviews.review_title: Very Basic - Don't waste your money.
reviews.review_description:
Audio trip is still the frontrunner! 
I thought beat sabre was bad but this is even worse. It's that basic, you barely need to move. I started on 'Hard' and had to go to show off to get any sort of exercise. Even my wife had to go on to the so called hard level. 
Just don't. 

Buy Audio Trip instead and save money. Good beats, familiar songs and 2 expansion packs with a 30min workout!

If you like exercising in time to music, this app works great! 

Prodigy songs Omen, and Take me to the hospital work very well.

On Quest 3, there seems to be a 10 minute limit on songs, so songs over that limit don't show up and aren't findable.

Hence 4 stars instead of 5.

I love that I can listen to my own music and I don’t have to pay for a “pack” to hear some musician . It can handle FLAC files no problem. Drawbacks are: it doesn’t make you move your feet. Not always well synchronized with the music. Could use some more backdrops. It takes a few steps to download a song but I don’t find it too tedious. Would be nice if you didn’t have to do it one by one.

It takes a little bit of work to get your songs downloaded and ready but it's a lot of fun. The visuals are basic but great looking. Best part is the exercise. Unless doing the exercise mode I don't feel anything. I finish playing with a sweat going. Glad I found and would recommend. I enjoyed this so much that I purchased in the past for my MR Headset and again for the Meta Quest Standalone.
I cNt seem to get my music on here, I've watched youtube after youtube video.

No other quest music game can play your own music. I've made a custom map in beatsaber before but it took a whole week, so if ur music isn't already mapped you're out of luck. Audioshield generates great maps in seconds.... Coming from beatsaber it's a breath of fresh air.

If you've a set of music you wanna play to and there's no custom maps for it in beatsaber, this is the game for you.

I have a modded quest and I had no neck issues unlike the other reviewers. Also I only use the quest 0-10 hours a week so that may also contribute.

reviews.review_title: Awesome when you load your own MP3
reviews.review_description: As soon as I bought this game I downloaded all the fast-paced MP3s I owned (since I mostly stream that was only about 80). I never did play this with the provided songs, but my daughter did. She echoed many of the reviews here that the game isn't as fun with the stock music. But when you're playing to songs you love, it's great.  Unlike some other games that allow you to play to your own music, AudioShield creates the play patterns straight from the music and it's ready to go as soon as you load it. The balls and actions are well-synchronized so it can feel like dancing. I pretty much always play it on the lower of the two exercise levels which involves ducking under triangles along with hitting the balls. I don't have any problems at all with the size of the shields or where the balls are coming from. This is actually one of my favorite games for exercise since every song I loaded is selectable.

I love how this game has many different modes so you can get used to the blue and red targets, then speed it up and add more obstacles. My favorite part of the game however, is the fact you can upload your own music and “workout” to it. I tried the xtreme mode and found that it got my legs feeling like I just ran a marathon! The easier modes are definitely better for just wanting to have fun and listen to your favorite songs, but the exercise modes make sure you get a good workout! I would recommend this to everyone!

Update: after playing just a couple of the extreme workouts, I woke up the next day feeling sore. This means the game definitely works for exercise!

To whom it may concern,

Things keep falling down from the top of my head and burst in front of my eyes. 
So that I need to keep raise my heavy head. 
And also so many light enter into my eyes. So make my eye so >< …
 Would you please regulate it. 
Thank you
Keep going

I am begging you PLEASE don’t buy this game! You will waste your money, I sure did! The custom music doesn’t work at all! It won’t even let me try and upload cause when I go to custom songs it just says the instructions but there is no way to get past that even after you’ve followed them! The graphics are very poor, this game is pointless! 

DEVELOPERS: Please help fix this problem or take it off the oculus store, thank you! (I will take down this bad review after my problem has been resolved)

AND DO NOT RESPOND WITH THE ISUE HAS BEEN FIXED LIKE YOU DO WITH EVERYONE ELSE BECAUSE IT HAS NOT BEEN FIXED!
This game sucks don't waste your money on it

reviews.review_title: Great exercise for an oldie like me!
reviews.review_description: This program has changed my life. I've tried other rhythm games, exercise videos on YouTube, etc., but most of them were too easy or too hard. AudioShield is perfect because I can change the difficulty by picking the right song. After some experimentation, I've got about twenty songs (from my own downloaded music library) that give me a good workout and track the rhythm and dynamics of the song in a fun way. My three favorites at the moment are One Thing Leads To Another (The Fixx), Word Up (Cameo), and Body Rock (Moby). If you want to just stand there and try for a perfect score, this game is probably not for you. But if you want to punch and sweep your arms with different intensities in a field wide enough to make you move your feet sometimes, get AudioShield. I'm still on Normal and will probably never use the other settings. I can really work up a sweat by punching harder and shifting my stance to see what's coming next. Or I can go easy with a slower song and make the punches more like dance moves. When I really get into the flow, it makes both my body and mind feel great! I highly recommend the setting that smooths the approach of the orbs and, because I'm short, I also tick the lower orbs box. This is the only aerobic exercise I've ever done that makes me sad when it's over. I'll go in thinking I'm just going to do five songs and I'll do ten instead! (For the record, I'm 55.)

Needs documentation on how to add songs using file transfer because downloading one song at a time with the browser is unrealistic.  And the file transfer method was non obvious to a newbie like me. 

Needs a shuffle songs forever option because stopping the action after every 3 or 4 minute song and then having to pick a new one is a pain. 

Good game, though!
After uploading songs I want to workout to,this game keeps me coming back several times a day. I really enjoy the workout
Love this app however , No one in my family can figure out how to get our own music on this app .. if any)me can help it would be greatly appreciated.
Use your own songs
This game I 200% recommend one of the best games on the quest and they have an amazing customer service team. I cannot recommend you get this game enough. Its awsome just absolutely amazing
Had trouble asking songs and realized that it can't be a very long song. Got it to work and was disappointed by the mapping. The song was upbeat but the targets moved very slow.
Tried to get a refund but they didn't approve.
